info.title: Topos OSS plane
info.description: The self-hostable Topos plane — workspace-credential writes AND reads (one Bearer credential per enrolled device; membership is the authorization), plus the enrollment + governance surface. Every returned protocol outcome of an op_id-carrying write rides in a 200 body (the canonical JsonEnvelope + receipt); non-2xx is reserved for transport/auth/integrity faults.

200.description: The claim bootstrap (workspace-to-be + plane API base; no bytes, no role, no trust root; enrollment_method "admin_claim", no skills). Content-negotiated: an Accept asking for JSON (or absent) gets this payload; anything else (curl `*/*`, a browser) gets a markdown agent-instruction document rendered from the same data, served as `text/plain` so browsers display it inline.

404.description: No such claim, or it is revoked/consumed/expired (always the JSON envelope, any Accept).

200.description: The admin-claim receipt — OK stands up the workspace + seats the first owner; DENIED the flat error.

400.description: Malformed body or device public key.

200.description: The device-authorization grant (device_code / user_code / verification_uri(_complete)) plus the plane block (API base + posture + method, no trust root) every start now carries.

400.description: Malformed body, device public key, workspace name, or intent/workspace combination.

404.description: A standup start on a plane that does not offer it (self-host).

200.description: The poll status (pending/slow_down/denied/expired/granted + the grant on granted).

404.description: No such device code.

200.description: A constant-shaped ack (the send is fire-and-forget; no enumeration oracle).

404.description: No live session for that user code.

200.description: The confirmation status (confirmed/wrong_code/expired/too_many_attempts).

404.description: No live session for that user code.

200.description: The verification-page disclosure (machine name, fingerprint, workspace, offered skills).

404.description: No live session for that user code.

200.description: The login receipt — OK carries the LoginData (the proven identity + one re-minted credential, or a blocked marker, per confirmed seat); DENIED the flat error.

400.description: Malformed body or device public key.

200.description: The proposal receipt (NEEDS_REVIEW on success; CONFLICT / DENIED / … otherwise).

404.description: Missing/blank credential.

200.description: The publish receipt — EVERY protocol outcome (OK / CONFLICT / APPROVAL_REQUIRED / DENIED / …).

404.description: Missing/blank credential.

200.description: The revert receipt (OK on success; CONFLICT / DENIED / … otherwise).

404.description: Missing/blank credential.

200.description: The review receipt (OK on an approve that promoted; CONFLICT / DENIED / … otherwise).

404.description: Missing/blank credential.

200.description: The workspace channels (the structural `everyone` included), with the caller's membership marked.

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

200.description: The membership outcome (joined, or a 200 DENIED CHANNEL_BUILTIN for `everyone`).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

200.description: The membership outcome (left / not_member, or a 200 DENIED CHANNEL_BUILTIN for `everyone`).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

200.description: The protect outcome (set, or a 200 DENIED REVIEWER_ROLE_REQUIRED / OWNER_ROLE_REQUIRED).

400.description: A level not valid for a channel (must be `curated` or `open`).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

parameters.name: ch
parameters.description: The channel name (created on first placement, member-level self-serve).

parameters.name: skill
parameters.description: The skill's immutable id to place into the channel.

200.description: The curation outcome (placed / created, or a 200 DENIED CURATED_ROLE_REQUIRED / BAD_NAME / SKILL_NOT_ACTIVE).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

parameters.name: skill
parameters.description: The skill's immutable id to remove from the channel.

200.description: The curation outcome (removed / not_placed, or a 200 DENIED CURATED_ROLE_REQUIRED).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

200.description: This device's delivery answer (entitled skills, detached, notices, open-proposal count).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

parameters.name: ws
parameters.description: Workspace id (the grant is authoritative for the workspace).

200.description: The redeem receipt — OK carries the registered device + minted read creds; DENIED the flat error.

parameters.name: ws
parameters.description: Workspace id (REST sugar; the authoritative workspace_id rides the body).

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: `Bearer <workspace credential>` — the acting device's credential (owner, or the target's own principal).

200.description: The revoke receipt — OK on success (instant revoke), DENIED (a 200) on a role denial.

parameters.name: skill
parameters.description: The followed skill's immutable id to exclude from THIS device.

200.description: The subscription outcome (excluded, or a 200 DENIED SKILL_NOT_ACTIVE).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

parameters.name: skill
parameters.description: The skill's immutable id to direct-follow (the client resolves the address to it).

200.description: The subscription outcome (followed, or a 200 DENIED SKILL_NOT_ACTIVE).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

parameters.name: skill
parameters.description: The skill's immutable id to unfollow (person-scoped negative mask).

200.description: The subscription outcome (unfollowed, or a 200 DENIED SKILL_NOT_ACTIVE).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

200.description: The invitation receipt — OK carries the InvitationData (address + invited + the honest mailed flag); a policy refusal is a 200 DENIED OWNER_ROLE_REQUIRED, an unknown channel a 200 DENIED UNKNOWN_CHANNEL.

400.description: Malformed body or a malformed invitee email.

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

200.description: The caller's own membership (identity + address + role + inviter + invite policy).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

200.description: The notices were acked (idempotent — only the caller's own unacked rows move).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

204.description: The policy was set (an idempotent write).

401.description: Admin token configured but missing/invalid on the request.

404.description: No admin token is configured on this plane (the route is disabled).

200.description: Every OPEN proposal in the workspace (the review inbox), author-message first.

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

204.description: The applied-state report was recorded (no body).

400.description: Malformed body or a bad skill / version id.

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

parameters.name: ws
parameters.description: Workspace id (REST sugar; the authoritative workspace_id rides the body).

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: The target principal whose role is set.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: `Bearer <workspace credential>` — the acting owner device's credential.

200.description: The roster-set receipt — OK on success, DENIED (a 200) on a role denial.

parameters.name: ws
parameters.description: Workspace id (REST sugar; the authoritative workspace_id rides the body).

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: The target principal to remove from the roster.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: `Bearer <workspace credential>` — the acting owner device's credential.

200.description: The roster-remove receipt — OK on success, DENIED (a 200) on a role denial.
The workspace skill catalog (metadata only; a possibly-empty list ordered by skill id).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

parameters.name: object_id
parameters.description: The content id (hex64) of the object to fetch.

200.description: The raw object bytes (content-addressed, immutable).

404.description: No/blank credential, scope/path mismatch, or unreachable object.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: `Bearer <workspace credential>` — the device's one workspace credential.

parameters.name: Topos-Known-Version-Id
parameters.description: The client's known current commit id (hex64) — the commit-sensitive half of the 304.

200.description: The current record (application/json) + a commit-sensitive ETag.

304.description: Pointer unchanged (the ETag AND the known version both match).

404.description: No such credential/membership/skill, or no current pointer yet.

parameters.name: skill
parameters.description: The skill's catalog name (or an archived successor's freed base name, or a bare skill id).

200.description: The skill's version history (purge tombstones included) + its proposal events.

404.description: Missing/blank credential, non-member, or unknown skill (indistinguishable).

200.description: The skill's OPEN, non-stale proposals (a possibly-empty list).

404.description: No/blank credential, non-member, or scope/path mismatch (indistinguishable).

200.description: The protect outcome (set, or a 200 DENIED REVIEWER_ROLE_REQUIRED / OWNER_ROLE_REQUIRED).

400.description: A level not valid for a skill (must be `reviewed` or `open`).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

200.description: The skill's audience (entitled members + their non-revoked devices).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, non-member, or unknown skill (indistinguishable).

parameters.name: version_id
parameters.description: The version (commit) id (hex64) whose metadata to read.

200.description: The version's authenticated metadata (immutable).

404.description: No/blank credential, scope/path mismatch, or unauthorized/unreachable version.

ActionCode.title: ActionCode
ActionCode.description: A machine-actionable next-action code. The values in `examples` are the known set; an unrecognized future code is still valid and MUST be executed via the action's `argv` (never rejected).

AddedNote.description:
The disclosure a `publish` attaches when it ADDED the skill to topos before shipping — the auto-add
convenience (`publish` accepts an untracked local source and adopts it first). Public disclosure only
(the same facts an explicit `topos add` would return); never a secret. **INFERRED** (additive-only).

harness_slug.description:
The harness registry slug the adopted directory was attributed to (e.g. `claude-code`), or `None`
for a plain directory adopted in place under no known harness.

name.description: The name the skill was adopted under (what `list` / `diff` / `publish` now resolve it by).

AdminClaimRequest.description:
`POST /v1/admin-claim` body — consume a one-time self-host claim token to stand up a workspace + seat its
first owner. The server re-derives the device key id from `device_public_key`.

device_public_key.description: The claiming device's raw 32-byte public key, base64url-unpadded.

display_name.description: The display name for the standing-up workspace.

Affected.description: What an outcome refers to (every field optional — a policy flip has no skill/version).

version.description: A `version_id` (commit SHA-256, lowercase hex) when the outcome refers to a version.

BootstrapData.description:
The payload an `/i/<token>` invite link resolves to — read once, before enrollment, to learn the
workspace + the plane API base to dial. No bytes, no role.

invite.description: The invite's non-secret descriptor (consent posture, expiry).

offered_skills.description:
The skills the invite pre-offers (a name may be absent → the client shows the id). Empty for a
membership-only door.

plane.description: The plane the workspace lives on — its API base and enrollment method.

schema_version.description: Always `1` for this contract version (the schema pins it `const`).

workspace.description: The workspace the invite is for.

BootstrapInvite.description: The invite's non-secret descriptor — the consent posture a redeemer agrees to, never a role.

consent.description: The consent posture every first-receive follows.

expires_at.description: The invite's expiry as an RFC-3339 string, if it expires (`None` = never).

first_receive_auto_land.description:
Whether a first-received skill auto-lands. **ALWAYS false** — a received skill is offered, never
silently applied (a direct human yes is required).

token_id.description:
A stable, non-secret reference for this invite (the link token the client used to reach the
payload). Empty for an admin-claim bootstrap — a claim token is a live one-time bearer capability
the server never echoes into a body; the claim client uses the token it parsed from the link.

BootstrapPlane.description: The plane a workspace lives on — its public base URL, deployment posture, and offered enrollment method.

base_url.description:
The plane's public API base URL — the root the client dials for enrollment and sync after this
bootstrap read. Always the plane itself, never a web front: a share link may ride another host,
but the client re-roots onto this value the moment the bootstrap is fetched.

enrollment_method.description: The enrollment method advertised to a bootstrapping device (e.g. `"device_code"` / `"passcode"`).

BootstrapSkill.description:
One skill an invite pre-offers — its id, plus an optional display name (names ride the invite; a missing
name means the client shows the id).

name.description: The skill's display name, if the invite carried one (else the client shows the id).

BootstrapWorkspace.description:
The workspace an invite is for — its id, display name, and domain-verification state. No role (the role
lives server-side on the pre-seeded member rows).

display_name.description: The workspace display name (shown on the verification page + in the agent's disclosure).

ConsentMode.description:
The consent posture of an invite — a CLOSED set. v0 is a single mode: a received skill is disclosed and
awaits a direct human yes on first receive (never auto-landed).

version_id.description:
The full commit SHA-256 (lowercase hex) — the `version_id`. NOT the `bundle_digest`: the
pointer names the version, and the commit transitively pins the bytes.

DeploymentMode.description:
A plane's deployment posture — a CLOSED set. `cloud` requires a confirmed identity step at enrollment;
`self_host` grants membership straight from a valid grant.

DeviceAuthorizeRequest.description:
`POST /v1/device/authorize` body — begin an RFC-8628 device-authorization flow. With a `workspace`
(the address name; the default `enroll` intent) the device enrolls toward that workspace; with
`intent = "standup"` — or neither field — it starts a STANDUP session (no workspace yet; hosted
planes only); with `intent = "login"` it starts a workspace-less LOGIN session whose grant redeems
at `POST /v1/login`. The server SERVER-derives the device key id from `device_public_key` (a
client-asserted id is never trusted).

device_public_key.description:
The device's raw 32-byte public key, base64url-unpadded. The server derives the device key id from
it (never a client-asserted id) and binds it to the enrollment session.

oneOf.description: The session intent. Absent defaults to `enroll` when a workspace is named, `standup` otherwise.

machine_name.description: A human-readable machine name shown on the verification page (a confused-deputy guard, not authority).

workspace.description:
The workspace ADDRESS name an enroll session targets (`topos.sh/<name>` minus the origin).
Whether the name exists is never disclosed on this route: an unknown name runs the same flow to
the same uniform denial a not-yours workspace gets.

DeviceAuthorizeResponse.description: `POST /v1/device/authorize` response — the RFC-8628 device-authorization grant (the names mirror the RFC).

device_code.description: The SECRET device code the client polls `device/token` with.

interval.description: The minimum poll interval, in seconds (RFC-8628 `interval`).

oneOf.description:
The plane block a STANDUP start carries (base URL, deployment posture, enrollment method) — a
standup device has no `/i/` bootstrap to learn these from. Absent on an enroll start.

user_code.description:
The opaque code identifying this device-auth session; it rides inside `verification_uri_complete`
(the human clicks the URL — it is not typed).

verification_uri.description: The verification URL a human visits to approve the session.

verification_uri_complete.description:
The verification URL with the user code already embedded (RFC-8628 `verification_uri_complete`) —
the one link to open; a client uses it VERBATIM when present.

DeviceRevokeRequest.description:
`DELETE /v1/workspaces/{ws}/devices` body — revoke a registered device key (owner, or the device's own
principal). The revoke is INSTANT (flip `revoked` in one transaction — the row's credential stops
authorizing fresh work the moment it commits).

target_device_key_id.description:
The id of the device key to revoke (the TARGET, named by its non-secret id; the actor rides the
Bearer credential).

workspace_id.description: The target workspace id (scopes the op to one workspace).

DeviceTokenRequest.description: `POST /v1/device/token` body — poll a device-authorization session for its grant.

DeviceTokenResponse.description:
`POST /v1/device/token` response — the poll `status`, plus the opaque single-use enrollment `grant` ONLY
when `status` is `granted`. A re-poll of a confirmed session re-derives the SAME grant (idempotent issue).

grant.description: The opaque single-use enrollment grant — present ONLY when `status` is `granted` (the redeem credential).

oneOf.description: The granted session's workspace context — present when `status` is `granted`.

DeviceTokenStatus.description:
A device-authorization poll status — the RFC-8628 outcomes (snake_case). `granted` carries the opaque
grant; every other status carries only itself (no grant).

DeviceTokenWorkspace.description:
The workspace context a `granted` poll carries — the id + display name a STANDUP client (which never
read an `/i/` bootstrap) needs to build its redeem possession frame and disclose what it joined.

address.description:
The workspace's full ADDRESS (server-built on the public link base) — the share line's root;
absent when the plane predates addresses or the grant is workspace-less.

display_name.description: The workspace display name (a disclosure aid; `""` if the plane no longer has one).

workspace_id.description: The workspace the grant is scoped to.

Generation.description:
The internal anti-replay counter `(epoch, seq)`. NEVER rendered to a user; the
agent consumes `expected`/`current` on a CONFLICT.

InvitationData.description:
`POST /v1/workspaces/{ws}/invitations` success `data` — what the inviter pastes onward: the workspace
ADDRESS (the whole invitation besides the roster rows themselves).

address.description: The workspace address (the share link — it carries nothing; the roster is the lock).

invited.description: The emails now seated as invited members (canonical form).

mailed.description: Whether invitation mail was sent server-side (`true` only when the server can send mail).

InvitationRequest.description:
`POST /v1/workspaces/{ws}/invitations` body — invitation as a ROSTER WRITE: seat each email as an
invited member (recording who invited whom) and optionally pre-place the person into channels.
There is no invite link and no role field — every CLI invitee starts as a member (roles are raised
later, on the web), and joining is `follow <address>` plus proof of the invited email. Member-level
unless the workspace's invite policy restricts inviting to owners.

channels.description:
Channel names to pre-place each invitee into (re-inviting restores placements; the structural
`everyone` needs no placement and is accepted as a no-op).

emails.description: The emails to seat as invited members (folded to the canonical lowercase form server-side).

JsonEnvelope.description:
The one common envelope every verb emits. Never prompts; prose (TTY) is rendered from the
SAME typed value where practical.

next_actions.description: Stable, machine-actionable next steps — each carries a complete argv.

oneOf.description: The durable idempotency record — present on **every** terminal op, including failures.

schema_version.description: Always `1` for this contract version (the schema pins it `const`).

LoginData.description: `POST /v1/login` success `data` — the proven identity + one entry per confirmed seat.

memberships.description: One entry per workspace where the principal holds a confirmed seat (possibly empty).

LoginMembership.description: One workspace a login re-minted (or could not re-mint) a credential for.

blocked.description: Why no credential was minted (e.g. this device is revoked in that workspace); absent on success.

credential.description:
The freshly minted plaintext workspace credential — absent when the mint was refused (see
`blocked`). Returned ONCE; the server stores only its hash.

device_key_id.description: The device's non-secret key id in this workspace (server-derived).

role.description: The person's role on the seat (`owner` / `reviewer` / `member`).

LoginRedeemRequest.description:
`POST /v1/login` body — redeem a LOGIN grant: prove the device key the grant is bound to and
receive one workspace credential per confirmed seat. The grant is the bearer credential; the
device public key is a binding check (nothing signs).

device_public_key.description: The device's raw 32-byte public key, base64url-unpadded — must equal the grant's bound key.

grant.description: The opaque grant from the login device flow (the bearer credential for this one exchange).

NextAction.description:
A machine-actionable next step. The `argv` is the ready-to-exec command; `code` lets an agent
branch on the known set and still pass through unknowns.

argv.description: A complete argv array — execute as-is (no TTY parsing).

NoticeAckRequest.description:
`POST /v1/workspaces/{ws}/notices/ack` body — acknowledge notices by id (person-scoped read-state;
the silent hook fetches without acking, an interactive narration acks what it narrated).

ids.description: The notice ids to mark read (only the caller's own unacked rows move; unknown ids are ignored).

PasscodeAck.description:
`POST /v1/enroll/passcode` response — a CONSTANT-shaped ack (always `sent`); the send is fire-and-forget,
so neither the body nor its latency reveals whether the address was rostered.

PasscodeAckStatus.description:
The constant-shaped status of a started passcode challenge — always `sent`, so a non-rostered address is
no enumeration oracle (the cloud roster gate is enforced at redeem, never here).

PasscodeConfirmRequest.description: `POST /v1/enroll/passcode/confirm` body — submit a passcode to confirm the session's identity.

email.description: The email the passcode was sent to.

user_code.description: The user code naming the live device-auth session.

PasscodeConfirmStatus.description:
The outcome of a passcode confirmation (snake_case). A wrong code carries only the status — never the
attempts remaining (no brute-force timing/count oracle on the wire).

PasscodeRequest.description: `POST /v1/enroll/passcode` body — start a passcode challenge for an email on a live device-auth session.

email.description: The email the passcode proves control of.

user_code.description: The user code naming the live device-auth session.

PolicyReviewRequiredRequest.description:
`PUT /v1/workspaces/{ws}/policy/review-required` body — the self-host operator toggle for the
`review-required` workspace policy (an idempotent set; JSON so the body stays extensible without a
path-shape change). Authenticated by the plane's admin token, not a device credential; the route is
invisible (404) on a plane with no admin token configured.

review_required.description: The desired policy value: `true` gates any direct publish behind a reviewer's approval.

ProposeData.description:
`publish --propose` (opens a PR; uploads a full candidate **without moving `current`**). Returns
`NEEDS_REVIEW`. **INFERRED.**

oneOf.description:
Present ONLY when THIS `publish --propose` invocation ADDED the skill to topos first (the auto-add
convenience — a proposal of an untracked local source adopts it before opening the PR). Discloses the
one local `add` the propose folded in; `None` when the skill was already tracked. **INFERRED.**

ProposeRequest.description:
`POST /v1/proposals` body — opens a proposal (a PR): ingests a full candidate **without moving
`current`** (`NEEDS_REVIEW`). The authority's `propose` op takes the **same** input shape as
`publish` (candidate + device + `op_id` + `expected`); there is **no** separate title/body on the op (a
title/body, if ever surfaced, would be composed into the candidate's commit message).

candidate.description: The full candidate bundle to ingest as the proposal's content.

channel.description:
The `--to` channel placement (same semantics as `PublishRequest.channel`): the placement
applies when the proposal opens — a placement is curation, gated by the channel's mode,
independent of the version's review.

display_name.description:
The skill's human display name (the author's skill-folder name) — ADVISORY metadata, carried for
symmetry with [`PublishRequest`]. It rides the proposal but is never digested or part of the
candidate; the plane records a name only when the pointer actually moves (a later approve/publish).

expected.description: The `(epoch, seq)` the proposal is born against (its base); a stale base later makes it non-current.

op_id.description: The client-minted UUIDv4 idempotency key (replays the stored receipt on retry).

skill_id.description: The target skill id within the workspace.

ProtectionSetRequest.description:
`PUT /v1/workspaces/{ws}/skills/{skill}/protection` and
`PUT /v1/workspaces/{ws}/channels/{ch}/protection` body — the safety knob. Levels are per kind:
`reviewed`/`open` for a skill bundle, `curated`/`open` for a channel. Tightening takes reviewer+;
loosening back to `open` takes an owner.

level.description: The target level (`open`, `reviewed` for skills, `curated` for channels).

PublishData.description:
`publish` (a direct publish that moves `current`). On a GENESIS (first) publish the client also folds in
a shareable `/i/` link pre-offering the skill — **best-effort + owner-gated** (minting it signs a
governance op the plane denies for a non-owner), so `invite_link` is `Some` only on a genesis publish by
an owner, and `None` otherwise. Under `review-required` a direct publish instead returns
`APPROVAL_REQUIRED` (with the `publish --propose` next-action) and carries no `data`. **INFERRED.**

An UN-ENROLLED direct publish on the hosted plane starts a workspace STANDUP instead of failing: the
envelope is still `ok = true`, but `data` carries the [`PublishPending`] block (sign in to approve) and
no version — `version_id` / `current_generation` are `None` at pending because nothing was published yet
(only the computed digest of the bytes being published can be honestly filled). Re-invoking the SAME
publish command (the `ENROLL_RESUME` next-action) resumes: once the sign-in is approved, the same command
completes enrollment AND the publish in one invocation, and the receipt carries the [`StandupReceipt`]
disclosure.

oneOf.description:
Present ONLY when THIS `publish` invocation ADDED the skill to topos first (the auto-add
convenience: `publish <name>|<name>@<harness>|<dir>` adopts an untracked LOCAL skill, then ships it
in one command). Discloses the one local `add` the publish folded in; `None` when the skill was
already tracked.

bundle_digest.description:
The byte-exact digest of the shipped (or, at pending, the scanned) bytes — always present: it is
computed over the draft before any network call, and an optional `<skill>@<digest>` pin gates it.

oneOf.description:
The pointer's new generation after the move — `None` while the publish is PENDING a standup sign-in
(no pointer moved yet).

Present when this publish is WAITING on the workspace-standup sign-in (the un-enrolled first publish
on a hosted plane): a human opens `verification_uri_complete` and approves; the agent then re-runs
the SAME publish command (the `ENROLL_RESUME` next-action carries the argv).

share_line.description:
The paste-able share line for the shipped skill (`<workspace address>/skills/<name>`) — present
when the plane disclosed the workspace address. The pointer move is the real outcome; the line is
what a human pastes in chat.

oneOf.description:
Present ONLY when THIS invocation completed a workspace standup before publishing — the disclosure
that makes a hijacked approval visible ("workspace X — owner Y"): the human who approved the sign-in
is the seated owner, so a name you don't recognize means someone else owns your workspace.

version_id.description:
The new commit — `None` while the publish is PENDING a workspace-standup sign-in (nothing was
published yet; the version id is only knowable once the bytes actually ship).

PublishPending.description: The workspace-standup sign-in a pending `publish` waits on. **INFERRED.**

device_fingerprint.description: 16-hex fingerprint of this device's public key; a human cross-checks it against the sign-in page.

expires_at.description: The sign-in session's expiry as an RFC-3339 string, if it expires.

status.description: Always `signin_required` — the one pending state this block discloses.

user_code.description: The code embedded in the URL, shown for cross-checking on the sign-in page.

verification_uri_complete.description:
The sign-in URL with the code already embedded — the ONE link a human opens to approve (served by
the plane; the client uses it verbatim).

PublishPendingStatus.description: A pending publish's status — a CLOSED single-value set (snake_case): the standup sign-in is required.

PublishRequest.description:
`POST /v1/publish` body — a direct publish that moves `current`. The acting device rides the
`Authorization: Bearer` workspace credential (resolved in-transaction to its registry row + the
membership gate); the server stamps `created_at`.
Under `review-required` the authority refuses this closed with `APPROVAL_REQUIRED`, ingesting nothing.

candidate.description: The full candidate bundle to ingest + publish.

channel.description:
The `--to` channel placement: place the skill's reference into this channel (created on first
use, member-level; a `curated` channel needs reviewer+ — the placement outcome rides the
receipt's details, independently of the version gate). Absent ⇒ no explicit placement (a
brand-new skill still lands in `everyone`).

display_name.description:
The skill's human display name (the author's skill-folder name) — ADVISORY metadata the plane
stores last-writer-wins and serves for display only (a follower names its folder by it; the
dashboard shows it). It is NOT part of the byte-exact bundle digest, the candidate, or the commit
id — a rename never changes a version id or digest. Absent ⇒ the plane keeps any existing name
(never clobbered to NULL).

expected.description: The `(epoch, seq)` this publish's compare-and-set targets; a stale pair yields `CONFLICT`.

op_id.description: The client-minted UUIDv4 idempotency key — the same `op_id` replays the stored receipt byte-for-byte.

skill_id.description: The target skill id within the workspace.

workspace_id.description: The target workspace id (the receipt + pointer scope).

Receipt.description:
The ONE canonical receipt across all outcomes — the durable idempotency record (present even
on failures): one stable receipt per op, identical on retry.

created_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp (the plane stamps it; never an ambient clock in `topos-core`).

op_id.description: Client-minted UUIDv4, persisted before the first send.

schema_version.description: Always `1` for this contract version (the schema pins it `const`).

RedeemRequest.description:
`POST /v1/workspaces/{ws}/devices` body — redeem an enrollment grant into a registered device + its
ONE minted workspace credential. The server checks `device_public_key` equals the grant's bound key
(a binding check) and re-derives the device key id from it.

device_public_key.description: The device's raw 32-byte public key, base64url-unpadded (must equal the grant's bound key).

grant.description: The opaque single-use enrollment grant (from a `granted` device-token poll).

workspace_id.description: The workspace the device enrolls into (authoritatively the grant's; echoed for the client's clarity).

RedeemResponse.description:
`POST /v1/workspaces/{ws}/devices` success payload — the confirmed enrollment: the registered device
and its ONE workspace credential (the `0600` at-rest secret this device presents on every read and
write in this workspace). **NO user token, ever; no per-skill token, ever.** Rides in the all-outcome
envelope's `data`.

credential.description:
The plaintext workspace credential (returned ONCE; only its sha256 is stored server-side, on the
device's registry row). No expiry — revocation is a directory row-write, re-enrollment the rotation.

device_key_id.description:
The server-derived device key id now registered (the device's stable, non-secret name — the
receipts/audit actor; never an authenticator).

principal.description:
The principal the device now acts as (the confirmed email, or a device-rooted id) — a disclosure the
client persists and prints so a hijacked standup is visible ("workspace X — owner Y").

workspace_id.description: The workspace the device enrolled into.

RevertData.description:
`revert` (a **forward** git-revert restoring older bytes as a new, higher-`seq` version — never a
pointer rollback, never a delete). `--to` names the GOOD version. **INFERRED.**

new_version_id.description: The new forward-revert commit that carries those bytes.

reverted_to.description: The good version named by `--to` (the bytes being restored).

RevertRequest.description:
`POST /v1/reverts` body — a **forward** revert: the server constructs a new 1-parent commit carrying the
`good` version's bytes on top of `current` (`seq` advances; the pointer never moves backward). There is
**no candidate** — the server reads `good`'s tree + digest from its provenance and builds the commit.

author.description: The author device id recorded in the forward-revert commit frame.

expected.description: The `(epoch, seq)` this revert's compare-and-set targets; a stale pair yields `CONFLICT`.

good.description: The GOOD version (the `version_id` whose bytes are restored) as 64-char lowercase hex.

op_id.description: The client-minted UUIDv4 idempotency key (replays the stored receipt on retry).

skill_id.description: The target skill id within the workspace.

ReviewData.description:
`review` (`--approve` / `--reject` a proposal). Approve is a compare-and-set on the base; a stale
base returns `CONFLICT`. **INFERRED.**

oneOf.description: The pointer's new generation when an approval moved `current`.

ReviewDecision.description:
A review verdict — `approve` promotes, `reject` carries a reason back, `withdraw` is the author
retracting their own open proposal. **INFERRED.**

ReviewRequest.description:
`POST /v1/reviews` body — a governance decision on an open proposal. `approve` runs the shared
`(epoch, seq)` compare-and-set on the proposal's base (a stale base ⇒ `CONFLICT`) and, under
`review_required`, enforces four-eyes (the proposer may not self-approve) before promoting; `reject`
is a standalone status flip (no pointer move).

decision.description:
The verdict — `approve` (promote), `reject` (with its reason), or `withdraw` (the author
retracting their own open proposal).

expected.description:
The `(epoch, seq)` the approval's compare-and-set targets (the proposal's base); a stale pair on an
`approve` yields `CONFLICT`.

op_id.description: The client-minted UUIDv4 idempotency key (replays the stored receipt on retry).

proposal.description: The proposal being reviewed, named by its candidate commit id (64-char lowercase hex).

reason.description: The reject's reason, carried back to the author (REQUIRED on `reject`; absent otherwise).

skill_id.description: The target skill id within the workspace.

RosterRemoveRequest.description:
`DELETE /v1/workspaces/{ws}/roster/{email}` body — remove a principal from the workspace roster
(owner-only). The target principal is the `{email}` path segment; the body carries only the op identity.

workspace_id.description: The target workspace id (scopes the op to one workspace).

RosterSetRequest.description:
`PUT /v1/workspaces/{ws}/roster/{email}` body — set a principal's workspace role (owner-only). The target
principal is the `{email}` path segment; the role rides the body.

role.description: The role to set on the `{email}` target.

workspace_id.description: The target workspace id (scopes the op to one workspace).

SessionIntent.description:
A device-auth session's intent — a CLOSED set (snake_case). `enroll` joins an existing workspace named
by its ADDRESS; `standup` starts with NO workspace (a signed-in human's approval creates one and seats
the approver as its first owner); `login` proves the person's identity and re-mints this device's
workspace credentials.

StandupReceipt.description:
The standup disclosure a workspace-creating publish carries: which workspace was stood up and who owns
it. **INFERRED.**

address.description: The stood-up workspace's ADDRESS (the share link — printed once at genesis, pasted anywhere).

owner_principal.description:
The seated owner principal (the approver's confirmed email, or a device-rooted id) — the hijack
tripwire: a principal you don't recognize means someone else approved (and owns) this workspace.

workspace_display_name.description: The stood-up workspace's display name (chosen at the sign-in approval).

TerminalOutcome.description: The closed set of terminal outcomes the agent branches on. SCREAMING_SNAKE on the wire.

VerificationContextResponse.description:
`GET /v1/enroll/verify/{user_code}` response — the verification-page disclosure a human reviews before
confirming an identity (the RFC-8628 confused-deputy guard). Carries no secret.

device_fingerprint.description:
A short hex fingerprint of the device's public key — a human cross-checks it against the device. A
display aid only, never an authority input.

oneOf.description:
The session's intent — `enroll` (join an existing workspace) or `standup` (approving CREATES one).
Absent from older planes ⇒ `enroll`; the page branches its copy on it.

machine_name.description: The human-readable machine name the device offered at start.

offered_skills.description: The skills the invite pre-offers (each with an optional display name).

workspace_display_name.description: The workspace display name the device would join.

VerifiedDomainStatus.description: A workspace's domain-verification state — snake_case on the wire.

WireAppliedReport.description:
`PUT /v1/workspaces/{ws}/report` body — the fleet's applied-state report: this device's
`(skill, applied version)` snapshot after a reconcile. The plane upserts the snapshot, drops the
non-detached rows it no longer names, and stamps the staleness clock (last-writer-wins; no receipt).
The acting device rides the `Authorization: Bearer` workspace credential, never a body field.

applied.description: The device's applied skills (possibly empty — a device holding nothing reports an empty list).

schema_version.description: Always `1` for this contract version (the schema pins it `const`).

WireAppliedSkill.description:
One applied-state row a device reports: the skill and the version it currently holds after its
reconcile.

version_id.description: The version this device holds (64-char lowercase hex).

WireCandidate.description:
A full candidate bundle: every file's bytes, the declared parents, and the author + message — the
shared input the `publish` and `propose` writes ingest (the `revert` write needs no candidate; the
server builds that forward commit from the good version). Maps to `plane-store`'s
`CandidateUpload { files, parents, author, message }` — each `parents` entry hex-decoded into a
`CommitId`.

author.description: The author device id recorded in the commit frame.

files.description: Every file in the candidate bundle (each server-rehashed).

message.description: The commit message (title + body composed into one string).

parents.description:
The candidate commit's declared parents, each a 64-char lowercase-hex `version_id` (`0` parents for a
genesis publish, `1` for a normal publish / propose, `2` for an author merge). Each must already be
present in the workspace; a lie changes the recomputed commit id, so the server need not trust it.

WireChannelEntry.description: One channel in the workspace's channel index.

builtin.description: Whether this is the structural `everyone` (roster-derived membership; cannot be joined or left).

member.description: Whether the CALLER is a member (always `true` on `everyone`).

member_count.description: How many people the channel reaches (confirmed roster size for `everyone`).

WireChannelIndex.description:
`GET /v1/workspaces/{ws}/channels` response — the workspace's channels with the caller's own
membership marked. Member-scoped.

WireChannelSkill.description: One skill reference inside a channel entry.

WireCurrentRecord.description:
The `current` pointer's wire body — the UNSIGNED document the plane serves at the pointer read,
embeds in OK receipts, and stores. It is unsigned: authority is the database row behind the pointer,
and integrity is the content-addressed `version_id` re-verified byte-for-byte on apply. A versioned
envelope; `ETag = "<epoch>.<seq>"`.

schema_version.description: Always `1` for this contract version (the schema pins it `const`).

WireDelivery.description:
`GET /v1/workspaces/{ws}/delivery` response body — the currency answer for ONE enrolled device: the
entitled skills (what this device should have), the person's detached skills (freeze-in-place, never
cleaned), the unacked notices feed, and the open-proposal count across the entitled set. The
session-start hook fetches it once per workspace and reconciles the harness against it, silently. A
versioned envelope.

detached.description:
The skill ids the person detached (unfollowed, or lapsed via a channel leave / removal) and that are
NOT currently re-entitled — every device freezes these in place, never cleaning them.

excluded.description:
The skill ids THIS DEVICE excludes ("not on this device") — the third actor in the who-acts
split, alongside the person (`detached`) and upstream (absent from `skills` entirely). The copy
leaves this device; the person keeps receiving it on every other device; `follow` here lifts it.
Sent so the client narrates the true CAUSE instead of mistaking an exclusion written elsewhere
(the web, a second tool) for an upstream withdrawal.

notices.description: The unacked, person-scoped notices (verdicts, proposal closures, …).

proposals_awaiting.description: The count of OPEN, non-stale proposals across the entitled skills (the review-inbox pressure gauge).

schema_version.description: Always `1` for this contract version (the schema pins it `const`).

skills.description: The entitled skills — everything this device should have (a possibly-empty list).

staleness_window_ms.description:
The workspace's staleness window (epoch milliseconds) — the ONE clock the fleet page and the
client's hook warning both read: a device whose last report is older than this is stale.
Additive: an older producer that omits it falls back to the one-week default (a whole delivery
body must never fail to parse over one new field).

workspace_id.description: The workspace this delivery is scoped to (echoed from the path).

WireDeliverySkill.description:
One skill THIS device should have, in the delivery answer: the catalog identity, the pinned `current`
version + its consent digest, the resolved protection posture, and the `via` attribution. **NO bytes** —
after reconciling the client fetches each version through the per-blob bundle read.

bundle_digest.description: The `current` byte-exact consent hash (64-char lowercase hex) — the fetch + re-hash pin.

display_name.description: The unsigned, advisory display name (the author's folder name); absent ⇒ show `name`.

kind.description:
The catalog's bundle kind — `"skill"` for everything that exists today. Display metadata
only: clients render it and never branch on it (an OPEN vocabulary, like `protection`).
Additive: an older producer that omits it is serving skills.

name.description: The catalog's user-facing name (the on-disk directory name for a fresh install).

protection.description:
The resolved per-bundle protection posture — `"open"` or `"reviewed"` (the client's publish
preflight; the server re-decides authoritatively on every write). An OPEN string for forward
compat — a client treats an unrecognized value as the stricter posture.

version_id.description: The pinned `current` version's commit id (64-char lowercase hex).

via.description: Why this device is entitled to the skill (channels ∪ direct).

WireError.description:
The flat wire error (rich `thiserror` enums stay internal). Carries a
stable code + retryability + the safe next actions; never raw SQL/git strings.

code.description:
A stable, machine-branchable error code, distinct from (and finer than) `outcome`. This is an
**open, additive** string vocabulary — it is intentionally NOT a closed enum: new codes are
added over time, and a consumer must treat an unrecognized `code` as the `outcome` it carries.

context.description: Structured context (no TTY prose to parse).

WireFile.description:
One file of a candidate bundle, uploaded **by value**. The raw bytes ride as base64 in the JSON body
(`content_base64`, standard alphabet); the server base64-decodes them and **rehashes every byte** to
derive the content id — there is no reference-by-id, and a client hash is never trusted. Maps to
`plane-store`'s `UploadedFile { path, mode, bytes }` (decode `content_base64` → `bytes`).

content_base64.description: The raw file bytes, base64-encoded. The server decodes then rehashes them (server-side digest).

WireFileMode.description:
A candidate file's mode on the wire — the two git regular-file modes as their literal octal strings
(`"100644"` / `"100755"`). A **closed** wire mirror of `topos_core::digest::FileMode`: that kernel enum
lives in a `no_std` crate `topos-types` does not depend on (and it carries no serde/schema derives), so
the wire leaf owns its own copy. The route handler maps it 1:1 at the edge —
`Regular ⇔ FileMode::Regular`, `Executable ⇔ FileMode::Executable` — for both the inbound candidate
([`WireFile`]) and the outbound version metadata ([`WireVersionFile`]).

WireLogProposal.description: One proposal event in a skill's history (open, and every resolution).

created_at.description: When the proposal was opened (server-stamped string).

resolved_at.description: When it was resolved (server-stamped string), when resolved.

resolved_reason.description: The resolution reason (a reject's rationale; a closure's cause), when recorded.

version_id.description: The proposed version's commit id (64-char lowercase hex).

WireLogVersion.description:
One version in a skill's history. A PURGED version stays listed — who purged it and when — with its
bytes gone (author/message may be absent once the underlying objects are reclaimed).

author.description: The version author, when the commit object is still readable.

current.description: Whether this version is the skill's `current`.

message.description: The version message, when the commit object is still readable.

purged_at.description: When the version's bytes were purged (epoch milliseconds) — the tombstone half.

purged_by.description: Who purged the version's bytes — the tombstone half.

version_id.description: The version's commit id (64-char lowercase hex).

WireMe.description:
`GET /v1/workspaces/{ws}/me` response — the caller's own membership describe: who you are here,
who added you, and the workspace's address block. Member-scoped (the uniform not-found otherwise).

address.description: The workspace's full address (the share link — server-built on the public link base).

invited_by.description: Who invited this principal (absent for a genesis owner).

WireNotice.description:
One unacked, person-scoped notice in the delivery feed. `kind` is an OPEN vocabulary that grows without
a schema break (today's values include `"verdict"` and `"proposal_closed"`); every other field is
present only when the notice names it. The silent currency hook fetches these without acking; an
interactive surface acks by id.

actor.description: The actor whose action raised the notice, when recorded.

created_at.description: When the notice was created (the server-stamped RFC-3339 string).

kind.description:
The notice kind — an OPEN vocabulary (e.g. `"verdict"`, `"proposal_closed"`); a client ignores a
kind it does not recognize.

message.description: A rendered human message, when the notice carries one.

outcome.description: The outcome (e.g. a verdict's `approve` / `reject`), when recorded.

reason.description: The human reason (a review's rationale), when recorded.

skill_id.description: The skill the notice concerns, when it names one.

skill_name.description: The skill's current catalog name (joined for narration), when the notice names a skill.

version_id.description: The version the notice concerns (64-char lowercase hex), when it names one.

WireOpenProposal.description:
One OPEN proposal on the wire — its candidate `version_id` (the `@hash`), the `base_generation` it was
opened against, and when. The proposals-listing read returns ONLY these three fields: **no bytes, no
proposer, no roles, no rendered diff**. Mirrors `plane-store`'s `OpenProposalSummary` with the id hex-encoded.

base_generation.description:
The `(epoch, seq)` the proposal was opened against (its base); when `current` advances past it the
proposal stales and drops out of this list.

created_at.description: When the proposal was opened (the server-stamped RFC-3339 string).

version_id.description: The proposal's candidate commit id (64-char lowercase hex) — the `<skill>@<version_id>` handle.

WireProposalEntry.description: One open proposal in the workspace's review inbox.

base_version_id.description: The base version the proposal was built on (64-char lowercase hex).

created_at.description: When the proposal was opened (the server-stamped string).

message.description: The author's message (the proposed version's commit message) — what a reviewer reads FIRST.

stale.description: Whether `current` has moved since the proposal's base (a stale proposal needs a re-propose).

version_id.description: The proposed version's commit id (64-char lowercase hex) — the review target handle.

WireProposalIndex.description:
`GET /v1/workspaces/{ws}/proposals` response — every OPEN proposal in the workspace, author-message
first. The caller splits inbox (others') from outbox (own) by `proposer`. Member-scoped.

WireProposalList.description:
`GET /v1/workspaces/{ws}/skills/{skill}/proposals` response body — the OPEN, non-stale proposals on a
rostered skill (a possibly-empty list, ordered by `(created_at, version_id)`). A staled proposal is absent
(keep == read == list); the list carries no bytes and no proposer — it is the thin handle a client turns
into a `diff` / `review` follow-up.

WireProtocolCard.description:
The MACHINE face of the constant protocol card — what an `Accept: application/json` GET of any
resource address returns, identical for every path (no content, no existence signal): just enough
for a client to re-root onto the API and run `follow`.

api_base_url.description: The API base URL the client re-roots onto (the origin serving `/v1`).

card.description: The constant discriminant a client dispatches on (`"topos-protocol-card"`).

schema_version.description: Always `1` for this contract version (the schema pins it `const`).

WireReach.description:
`GET /v1/workspaces/{ws}/skills/{skill}/reach` response — the audience a change to this skill
reaches (the describe number behind `publish` and `protect`). Member-scoped.

devices.description: How many registered, non-revoked devices those people hold here.

persons.description: How many people are entitled to the skill.

WireSkillIndex.description:
`GET /v1/workspaces/{ws}/skills` response body — the workspace catalog (every skill holding a `current`),
authorized by workspace membership (the `Authorization: Bearer` workspace credential names the
device; catalog
visibility == membership, on both cloud and self-host). Metadata only, no bytes; a possibly-empty list
ordered by `skill_id`.

WireSkillIndexEntry.description:
One skill of the workspace catalog, as `GET /v1/workspaces/{ws}/skills` returns it: the discovery
metadata a member browses to decide what to follow — **NO bytes**. Mirrors `plane-store`'s
`SkillIndexRow` with every 32-byte id hex-encoded.

bundle_digest.description: The `current` byte-exact consent hash (64-char lowercase hex).

display_name.description: The unsigned, advisory folder display name (may be absent).

kind.description:
The catalog's bundle kind — `"skill"` for everything that exists today. Display metadata
only: clients render it and never branch on it (an OPEN vocabulary, like `status`).
Additive: an older producer that omits it is serving skills.

name.description: The catalog's user-facing name (a pre-catalog seeded pointer falls back to the skill id).

open_proposals.description: The count of OPEN, non-stale proposals on the skill.

status.description:
The catalog lifecycle status — `"active"` / `"archived"` (a deleted skill has no `current` row and
so no entry). An OPEN string, deliberately: a new state can land without a schema break.

version_id.description: The `current` version's commit id (64-char lowercase hex).

WireSkillLog.description:
`GET /v1/workspaces/{ws}/skills/{skill}/log` response — the skill's version history (purge
tombstones included) and its proposal events. An ARCHIVED skill stays addressable here; asking by a
freed base name answers the archived successor as a hint. Member-scoped.

base_name.description: The pre-archive name, when archived (what the skill was called before the rename freed it).

kind.description:
The catalog's bundle kind — `"skill"` for everything that exists today. Display metadata
only: clients render it and never branch on it. Additive: an older producer omits it.

name.description: The skill's current catalog name (the archived spelling once archived).

versions.description:
The versions, newest first where orderable (the ancestry walk stops at reclaimed history;
tombstoned leftovers follow unordered).

WireVersionFile.description:
One file of a version's metadata on the wire — its path, mode, and content id (`object_id`), mirroring
`plane-store`'s `VersionFile` with the id hex-encoded. The **bytes are NOT here**: a client fetches each
by `object_id` through the bundle (object) read route.

object_id.description: The file's content id (64-char lowercase hex) — the handle the per-blob read route resolves.

WireVersionMeta.description:
`GET /v1/workspaces/{ws}/skills/{skill}/versions/{version_id}` response body — a version's authenticated
metadata: its id, the COMPLETE parent set, display author + message, the consent `bundle_digest`, and the
per-file `(path, mode, object_id)` leaves. Mirrors `plane-store`'s `VersionMeta` with every 32-byte id
hex-encoded. Assembled WITHOUT reading any blob bytes; the `bundle_digest` is the pin the client's
per-blob fetches + its own re-hash must reproduce.

author.description: The author device id from the commit frame.

bundle_digest.description: The byte-exact consent hash over the bundle (64-char lowercase hex) — the fetch + re-hash pin.

files.description: The per-file leaves, in the version's recorded order.

message.description: The commit message (title + body as one string).

parents.description:
The COMPLETE parent set, each a 64-char lowercase-hex commit id (`0` for genesis, `1` normally, `2`
for an author merge).

version_id.description: This version's commit id (64-char lowercase hex).

WireVia.description:
Why THIS device is entitled to a delivered skill — the attribution the client's narration reads. A
delivered skill always has at least one of the two: it rides one or more channels, and/or the person
follows it directly (a direct follow survives every channel drop).

channels.description: The channels delivering the skill (names, sorted; `everyone` is present when it delivers).

direct.description: Whether the person also follows the skill directly (independent of any channel).

WorkspaceRole.description: A workspace-level governance role (the RBAC roster — DISTINCT from the per-skill read roster). snake_case.

tags.name: writes
tags.description: Device-credential writes (publish / propose / revert / review) and the member-lane row ops (follows / channels / exclusions / protection / notices).

tags.name: reads
tags.description: Workspace-credential device reads (current / bundles / versions / proposals / catalog / delivery / me / channels / proposals / log / reach) plus the body-light applied-state report.

tags.name: enrollment
tags.description: Claim bootstrap + the device-auth / passcode / redeem / admin-claim / login enrollment flow.

tags.name: governance
tags.description: Owner/admin device-credential mutations (roster / revoke) and the member-lane invitation (a roster write).
